title: gate-events.log/overrides.log cannot attribute an entry to Claude or Codex despite both hosts sharing one audit trail
evidence: _log_gate_event(kind, tag, msg) builds its line as `f"[{ts}] {kind} {tag_part}hook={hook} | {msg}\n"` where `hook = os.path.basename(sys.argv[0])` (line 686). Both plugins invoke the same script basenames — hooks.json for ca-codex runs `python3 \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/pre-write.py\"` (plugins/ca-codex/hooks/hooks.json:34) exactly as plugins/ca/hooks.json does — so basename('.../ca/hooks/pre-write.py') == basename('.../ca-codex/hooks/pre-write.py') == 'pre-write.py'. The host-abstraction seam (hostapi.get_host().name, values 'claude'/'codex' per core/pysrc/hostapi.py:43 and plugins/ca-codex/hooks/_host.py:151) is loaded by every entry script (_hooklib.get_host(), _hooklib.py:79-84) but _log_gate_event never reads it — no host field is threaded into the log line.
impact: gate-events.log and overrides.log are the durable audit trail for BLOCK/REMIND/WARN and override decisions (inventory.md boundary #3: 'two hosts may read/write the same state dir concurrently'). When a Claude session and a Codex session touch the same repo, every line in the shared log is indistinguishable by host of origin. A maintainer investigating 'did the Codex gate actually fire, or did Claude Code fire it and Codex stayed dormant' (the exact M2 verification goal per the codex-support-campaign) cannot answer that from the audit trail alone — they'd need to correlate against out-of-band process/session metadata that doesn't exist anywhere in the log.
recommendation: Thread get_host().name (or a short host tag) into _log_gate_event's line format, e.g. `hook=<script> host=<name>`, so forensic reconstruction of a shared log doesn't require external correlation.
acceptance_criteria: A BLOCK/REMIND/WARN emitted from a ca-codex-installed hook produces a gate-events.log line distinguishable (via an explicit host field) from the same event emitted from the ca-installed hook.
Existing gate-events.log consumers (metrics.py, doctor.py, any parser of the fixed line grammar) are updated or shown compatible with the added field.
